<content>1: package com.mzfuture.entire.gitsync.service.impl;
2: 
3: import cn.hutool.core.util.StrUtil;
4: import com.mzfuture.entire.config.GitProperties;
5: import com.mzfuture.entire.gitrepo.entity.Repo;
6: import com.mzfuture.entire.gitrepo.enums.RepositoryPlatform;

11: import com.mzfuture.entire.gitsync.dto.response.GitSyncResult;
12: import com.mzfuture.entire.gitsync.dto.response.TokenValidateResult;
13: import com.mzfuture.entire.gitsync.dto.response.PullResult;
14: import com.mzfuture.entire.gitsync.enums.GitSyncStatus;
15: import com.mzfuture.entire.gitsync.adapter.GitPlatformAdapter;
16: import com.mzfuture.entire.gitsync.service.GitOperationService;
17: import com.mzfuture.entire.gitsync.service.GitSyncService;
18: import com.mzfuture.entire.common.exception.Errors;
19: import lombok.extern.slf4j.Slf4j;
20: import org.springframework.stereotype.Service;
21: 
22: import java.util.List;
23: import java.util.Map;
24: import java.util.function.Supplier;
25: import java.util.stream.Collectors;
26: 
27: /// Git sync service implementation
28: @Slf4j
29: @Service
30: public class GitSyncServiceImpl implements GitSyncService {
31: 
32:     private final RepoRepository repoRepository;
33:     private final GitOperationService gitOperationService;
34:     private final GitProperties gitProperties;
35:     private final Map<RepositoryPlatform, GitPlatformAdapter> platformAdapters;
36: 
37:     public GitSyncServiceImpl(
38:             RepoRepository repoRepository,
39:             GitOperationService gitOperationService,
40:             GitProperties gitProperties,
41:             List<GitPlatformAdapter> adapters) {
42:         this.repoRepository = repoRepository;
43:         this.gitOperationService = gitOperationService;
44:         this.gitProperties = gitProperties;
45:         this.platformAdapters = adapters.stream()
46:                 .collect(Collectors.toMap(GitPlatformAdapter::getPlatform, adapter -> adapter));
47:     }
48: 
49:     @Override
50:     public GitSyncResult syncRepository(GitSyncParams params) {
51:         // 1. Match repository exactly by webUrl
52:         Repo repo = repoRepository.findByWebUrl(params.getWebUrl())
53:                 .orElseThrow(() -> Errors.NOT_FOUND.toException("Repository not found: " + params.getWebUrl()));
54: 
55:         // 2. Execute sync (using global default depth)
56:         return doSync(repo, params.getAccessToken(), params.getBranch(), -1);
57:     }
58: 
59:     @Override
60:     public GitSyncResult syncRepositoryById(Long repoId) {
61:         Repo repo = repoRepository.findById(repoId)
62:                 .orElseThrow(() -> Errors.NOT_FOUND.toException("Repository does not exist: " + repoId));
63:         return doSync(repo, null, null, -1);
64:     }
65: 
66:     @Override
67:     public GitSyncResult syncRepositoryById(Long repoId, String branch, int cloneDepth) {
68:         Repo repo = repoRepository.findById(repoId)
69:                 .orElseThrow(() -> Errors.NOT_FOUND.toException("Repository does not exist: " + repoId));
70:         return doSync(repo, null, branch, cloneDepth);
71:     }
72: 
73:     /// Execute sync operation; cloneDepth < 0 means use global default depth
74:     private GitSyncResult doSync(Repo repo, String requestToken, String requestBranch, int cloneDepth) {
75:         Long repoId = repo.getId();
76:         String repoName = repo.getName();
77:         String webUrl = repo.getWebUrl();
78:         RepositoryPlatform platform = repo.getPlatform();
79: 
80:         // Check if platform is supported
81:         GitPlatformAdapter adapter = platformAdapters.get(platform);
82:         if (adapter == null) {
83:             throw Errors.INTERNAL_ERROR.toException("Unsupported platform type: " + platform);
84:         }
85: 
86:         // Prefer token from request, otherwise use token from database
87:         String accessToken = StrUtil.isNotBlank(requestToken) ? requestToken : repo.getAccessToken();
88:         if (StrUtil.isBlank(accessToken)) {
89:             throw Errors.INTERNAL_ERROR.toException("Access token cannot be empty");
90:         }
91: 
92:         // Determine branch (use default branch when requestBranch is empty and cloneDepth is not specified)
93:         String branch = StrUtil.isNotBlank(requestBranch) ? requestBranch : gitProperties.getDefaultBranch();
94: 
95:         // Build auth URL
96:         String authUrl = adapter.buildAuthUrl(webUrl, accessToken);
97: 
98:         log.info("Starting repository sync: repoId={}, name={}, platform={}, branch={}",
99:                 repoId, repoName, platform, branch);
100: 
101:         // Determine if clone or pull
102:         boolean exists = gitOperationService.isRepositoryExists(repoId);
103: 
104:         GitSyncResult result = new GitSyncResult();
105:         result.setRepoId(repoId);
106:         result.setRepoName(repoName);
107:         result.setBranch(branch);
108:         result.setLocalPath(gitOperationService.getLocalRepositoryPath(repoId));
109:         result.setSyncTime(System.currentTimeMillis());
110: 
111:         try {
112:             if (exists) {
113:                 // Execute pull (with retry)
114:                 PullResult pullResult = executeWithRetry(() ->
115:                         gitOperationService.pullRepository(repoId, authUrl, branch));
116: 
117:                 result.setStatus(GitSyncStatus.SUCCESS.name());
118:                 result.setHasUpdate(pullResult.isUpdated());
119:                 result.setCommitId(pullResult.getCommitId());
120:                 result.setCommitMessage(pullResult.getCommitMessage());
121: 
122:                 log.info("Repository sync successful (pull): repoId={}, hasUpdate={}",
123:                         repoId, pullResult.isUpdated());
124: 
125:             } else {
126:                 // Execute clone (with retry); cloneDepth < 0 uses default depth
127:                 int depth = cloneDepth < 0 ? gitProperties.getCloneDepth() : cloneDepth;
128:                 final int finalDepth = depth;
129:                 executeWithRetry(() -> {
130:                     gitOperationService.cloneRepository(repoId, authUrl, branch, finalDepth);
131:                     return null;
132:                 });
133: 
134:                 // Get repository status
135:                 var status = gitOperationService.getRepositoryStatus(repoId);
136: 
137:                 result.setStatus(GitSyncStatus.SUCCESS.name());
138:                 result.setHasUpdate(true);
139:                 result.setCommitId(status.getCommitId());
140:                 result.setCommitMessage(status.getCommitMessage());
141: 
142:                 log.info("Repository sync successful (clone): repoId={}, path={}",
143:                         repoId, result.getLocalPath());
144:             }
145: 
146:         } catch (Exception e) {
147:             log.error("Repository sync failed: repoId={}, error={}", repoId, e.getMessage(), e);
148: 
149:             result.setStatus(GitSyncStatus.FAILED.name());
150:             result.setErrorMessage(e.getMessage());
151:             result.setHasUpdate(false);
152: 
153:             throw Errors.INTERNAL_ERROR.toException("Repository sync failed: " + e.getMessage());
154:         }
155: 
156:         return result;
157:     }
158: 
159:     /// Execute operation with retry
160:     private <T> T executeWithRetry(Supplier<T> operation) {
161:         int maxRetries = gitProperties.getRetryCount();
162:         int attempt = 0;
163:         Exception lastException = null;
164: 
165:         while (attempt <= maxRetries) {
166:             try {
167:                 if (attempt > 0) {
168:                     log.info("Retry attempt {}...", attempt);
169:                     // Wait 1 second before retry
170:                     Thread.sleep(1000);
171:                 }
172:                 return operation.get();
173:             } catch (Exception e) {
174:                 lastException = e;
175:                 attempt++;
176:                 log.warn("Operation failed (attempt {}): {}", attempt, e.getMessage());
177:             }
178:         }
179: 
180:         throw Errors.INTERNAL_ERROR.toException(
181:                 "Operation failed after " + maxRetries + " retries: " + lastException.getMessage());
182:     }
183: 
184:     @Override
185:     public GitBranchesDTO getBranches(Long repoId) {
186:         Repo repo = repoRepository.findById(repoId)
187:                 .orElseThrow(() -> Errors.NOT_FOUND.toException("Repository does not exist: " + repoId));
188: 
189:         GitPlatformAdapter adapter = platformAdapters.get(repo.getPlatform());
190:         if (adapter == null) {
191:             throw Errors.INTERNAL_ERROR.toException("Unsupported platform type: " + repo.getPlatform());
192:         }
193: 
194:         if (StrUtil.isBlank(repo.getAccessToken())) {
195:             throw Errors.INTERNAL_ERROR.toException("Access token cannot be empty");
196:         }
197: 
198:         String authUrl = adapter.buildAuthUrl(repo.getWebUrl(), repo.getAccessToken());
199: 
200:         List<String> branches = gitOperationService.listRemoteBranches(authUrl);
201: 
202:         GitBranchesDTO result = new GitBranchesDTO();
203:         result.setRepoId(repoId);
204:         result.setBranches(branches);
205:         return result;
206:     }
207: 
208:     @Override
209:     public GitSyncResult syncRepositoryAllBranchesForCheckpoint(Long repoId, String metadataBranch, int cloneDepth) {
210:         Repo repo = repoRepository.findById(repoId)
211:                 .orElseThrow(() -> Errors.NOT_FOUND.toException("Repository does not exist: " + repoId));
212:         GitPlatformAdapter adapter = platformAdapters.get(repo.getPlatform());
213:         if (adapter == null) {
214:             throw Errors.INTERNAL_ERROR.toException("Unsupported platform type: " + repo.getPlatform());
215:         }
216:         if (StrUtil.isBlank(repo.getAccessToken())) {
217:             throw Errors.INTERNAL_ERROR.toException("Access token cannot be empty");
218:         }
219:         String authUrl = adapter.buildAuthUrl(repo.getWebUrl(), repo.getAccessToken());
220:         List<String> remoteBranches = gitOperationService.listRemoteBranches(authUrl);
221:         if (remoteBranches == null || remoteBranches.isEmpty()) {
222:             throw Errors.INTERNAL_ERROR.toException("No remote branches");
223:         }
224: 
225:         GitSyncResult result = new GitSyncResult();
226:         result.setRepoId(repoId);
227:         result.setRepoName(repo.getName());
228:         result.setBranch(metadataBranch);
229:         result.setLocalPath(gitOperationService.getLocalRepositoryPath(repoId));
230:         result.setSyncTime(System.currentTimeMillis());
231: 
232:         boolean exists = gitOperationService.isRepositoryExists(repoId);
233:         try {
234:             if (exists) {
235:                 gitOperationService.fetchBranches(repoId, authUrl, remoteBranches);
236:                 result.setStatus(GitSyncStatus.SUCCESS.name());
237:                 result.setHasUpdate(true);
238:                 log.info("checkpoint all branches fetch completed: repoId={}, branches={}", repoId, remoteBranches.size());
239:             } else {
240:                 String cloneBranch = remoteBranches.contains(metadataBranch) ? metadataBranch : remoteBranches.get(0);
241:                 int depth = cloneDepth < 0 ? gitProperties.getCloneDepth() : cloneDepth;
242:                 executeWithRetry(() -> {
243:                     gitOperationService.cloneRepository(repoId, authUrl, cloneBranch, depth);
244:                     return null;
245:                 });
246:                 gitOperationService.fetchBranches(repoId, authUrl, remoteBranches);
247:                 result.setStatus(GitSyncStatus.SUCCESS.name());
248:                 result.setHasUpdate(true);
249:                 log.info("checkpoint clone + all branches fetch completed: repoId={}, cloneBranch={}, branches={}",
250:                         repoId, cloneBranch, remoteBranches.size());
251:             }
252:         } catch (Exception e) {
253:             log.error("checkpoint all branches sync failed: repoId={}, error={}", repoId, e.getMessage(), e);
254:             result.setStatus(GitSyncStatus.FAILED.name());
255:             result.setErrorMessage(e.getMessage());
256:             result.setHasUpdate(false);
257:             throw Errors.INTERNAL_ERROR.toException("checkpoint all branches sync failed: " + e.getMessage());
258:         }
259:         return result;
260:     }
261: 
262:     @Override
263:     public void fetchMetadataBranch(Long repoId, String metadataBranch) {
264:         Repo repo = repoRepository.findById(repoId)
265:                 .orElseThrow(() -> Errors.NOT_FOUND.toException("Repository does not exist: " + repoId));
266:         GitPlatformAdapter adapter = platformAdapters.get(repo.getPlatform());
267:         if (adapter == null) {
268:             throw Errors.INTERNAL_ERROR.toException("Unsupported platform type: " + repo.getPlatform());
269:         }
270:         if (StrUtil.isBlank(repo.getAccessToken())) {
271:             throw Errors.INTERNAL_ERROR.toException("Access token cannot be empty");
272:         }
273:         String authUrl = adapter.buildAuthUrl(repo.getWebUrl(), repo.getAccessToken());
274:         gitOperationService.fetchBranches(repoId, authUrl, List.of(metadataBranch));
275:         log.debug("checkpoint metadata branch already fetched to latest: repoId={}, branch={}", repoId, metadataBranch);
276:     }
277: 
278:     @Override
279:     public TokenValidateResult validateToken(TokenValidateParams params) {
280:         GitPlatformAdapter adapter = platformAdapters.get(params.getPlatform());
281:         if (adapter == null) {

34: @Service
35: public class GitOperationServiceImpl implements GitOperationService {
36: 
37:     private final GitProperties gitProperties;
38: 
39:     // Repository operation lock, preventing concurrent operations on the same repository
40:     private final ConcurrentHashMap<Long, ReentrantLock> repoLocks = new ConcurrentHashMap<>();
41: 
42:     public GitOperationServiceImpl(GitProperties gitProperties) {
43:         this.gitProperties = gitProperties;
44:     }
45: 
46:     @Override
47:     public String cloneRepository(Long repoId, String authUrl, String branch) {
48:         return cloneRepository(repoId, authUrl, branch, 0);
49:     }
50: 
51:     @Override
52:     public String cloneRepository(Long repoId, String authUrl, String branch, int depth) {
53:         ReentrantLock lock = getRepoLock(repoId);
54:         lock.lock();
55:         try {
56:             String localPath = getLocalRepositoryPath(repoId);
57:             File repoDir = new File(localPath);
58: 
59:             // If directory already exists, delete first
60:             if (repoDir.exists()) {
61:                 FileUtil.del(repoDir);
62:             }
63: 
64:             // Create directory
65:             repoDir.mkdirs();
66: 
67:             int effectiveDepth = depth <= 0 ? gitProperties.getCloneDepth() : depth;
68:             log.info("Starting to clone repository: repoId={}, branch={}, path={}, depth={}", repoId, branch, localPath, effectiveDepth);
69: 
70:             // Parse token for authentication
71:             CredentialsProvider credentials = parseCredentials(authUrl);
72: 
73:             CloneCommand clone = Git.cloneRepository()
74:                     .setURI(authUrl)
75:                     .setDirectory(repoDir)
76:                     .setBranch(branch)
77:                     .setBranchesToClone(List.of("refs/heads/" + branch))
78:                     .setTimeout(gitProperties.getTimeoutSeconds());
79:             if (effectiveDepth > 0) {
80:                 clone.setDepth(effectiveDepth);
81:             }
82: 
83:             if (credentials != null) {
84:                 clone.setCredentialsProvider(credentials);
85:             }
86: 
87:             clone.call();
88: 
89:             log.info("Repository cloned successfully: repoId={}, path={}", repoId, localPath);
90:             return localPath;
91: 
92:         } catch (GitAPIException e) {
93:             log.error("Failed to clone repository: repoId={}, error={}", repoId, e.getMessage(), e);
94:             throw Errors.INTERNAL_ERROR.toException("Failed to clone repository: " + e.getMessage());
95:         } finally {
96:             lock.unlock();
97:         }
98:     }
99: 
100:     @Override
101:     public PullResult pullRepository(Long repoId, String authUrl, String branch) {
102:         ReentrantLock lock = getRepoLock(repoId);
103:         lock.lock();
104:         try {
105:             String localPath = getLocalRepositoryPath(repoId);
106:             File repoDir = new File(localPath);
107: 
108:             if (!repoDir.exists()) {
109:                 throw Errors.INTERNAL_ERROR.toException("Local repository does not exist: " + localPath);
110:             }
111: 
112:             log.info("Starting to pull repository updates: repoId={}, branch={}", repoId, branch);
113: 
114:             // Parse token for authentication
115:             CredentialsProvider credentials = parseCredentials(authUrl);
116: 
117:             try (Git git = Git.open(repoDir)) {
118:                 // First checkout target branch
119:                 git.checkout().setName(branch).call();
120: 
121:                 // Pull updates
122:                 PullCommand pull = git.pull()
123:                         .setRemoteBranchName(branch)
124:                         .setTimeout(gitProperties.getTimeoutSeconds());
125: 
126:                 if (credentials != null) {
127:                     pull.setCredentialsProvider(credentials);
128:                 }
129: 
130:                 org.eclipse.jgit.api.PullResult pullResult = pull.call();
131: 
132:                 // Get latest commit information
133:                 RevCommit latestCommit = getLatestCommit(git);
134: 
135:                 boolean hasUpdate = pullResult.getMergeResult() != null
136:                         && pullResult.getMergeResult().getMergeStatus().isSuccessful();
137: 
138:                 log.info("Repository pull completed: repoId={}, hasUpdate={}", repoId, hasUpdate);
139: 
140:                 return PullResult.builder()
141:                         .updated(hasUpdate)
142:                         .commitId(latestCommit != null ? latestCommit.getName() : null)
143:                         .commitMessage(latestCommit != null ? latestCommit.getShortMessage() : null)
144:                         .author(latestCommit != null ? latestCommit.getAuthorIdent().getName() : null)
145:                         .commitTime(latestCommit != null ? latestCommit.getCommitTime() * 1000L : null)
146:                         .build();
147:             }
148: 
149:         } catch (IOException | GitAPIException e) {
150:             log.error("Failed to pull repository: repoId={}, error={}", repoId, e.getMessage(), e);
151:             throw Errors.INTERNAL_ERROR.toException("Failed to pull repository: " + e.getMessage());
152:         } finally {
153:             lock.unlock();
154:         }
155:     }
156: 
157:     @Override
158:     public GitStatusDTO getRepositoryStatus(Long repoId) {
159:         String localPath = getLocalRepositoryPath(repoId);
160:         File repoDir = new File(localPath);
161: 
162:         GitStatusDTO status = new GitStatusDTO();
163:         status.setRepoId(repoId);
164:         status.setLocalPath(localPath);
165:         status.setExists(repoDir.exists() && new File(localPath + "/.git").exists());
166: 
167:         if (!status.getExists()) {
168:             return status;
169:         }
170: 
171:         try (Git git = Git.open(repoDir)) {
172:             Repository repository = git.getRepository();
173: 
174:             // Get current branch
175:             Ref head = repository.exactRef("HEAD");
176:             if (head != null && head.isSymbolic()) {
177:                 String branchName = Repository.shortenRefName(head.getTarget().getName());
178:                 status.setCurrentBranch(branchName);
179:             }
180: 
181:             // Get latest commit
182:             RevCommit latestCommit = getLatestCommit(git);
183:             if (latestCommit != null) {
184:                 status.setCommitId(latestCommit.getName());
185:                 status.setCommitMessage(latestCommit.getShortMessage());
186:                 status.setLastCommitTime(latestCommit.getCommitTime() * 1000L);
187:             }
188: 
189:         } catch (IOException e) {
190:             log.warn("Failed to get repository status: repoId={}, error={}", repoId, e.getMessage());
191:         }
192: 
193:         return status;
194:     }
195: 
196:     @Override
197:     public boolean isRepositoryExists(Long repoId) {
198:         String localPath = getLocalRepositoryPath(repoId);
199:         File repoDir = new File(localPath);
200:         File gitDir = new File(localPath + "/.git");
201:         return repoDir.exists() && gitDir.exists();
202:     }
203: 
204:     @Override
205:     public String getLocalRepositoryPath(Long repoId) {
206:         String dataPath = gitProperties.getDataPath();
207:         // Ensure path ends with /
208:         if (!dataPath.endsWith("/") && !dataPath.endsWith("\\")) {
209:             dataPath = dataPath + "/";
210:         }
211:         return dataPath + repoId;
212:     }
213: 
214:     @Override
215:     public boolean deleteRepository(Long repoId) {
216:         ReentrantLock lock = getRepoLock(repoId);
217:         lock.lock();
218:         try {
219:             String localPath = getLocalRepositoryPath(repoId);
220:             File repoDir = new File(localPath);
221: 
222:             if (!repoDir.exists()) {
223:                 return true;
224:             }
225: 
226:             log.info("Deleting local repository: repoId={}, path={}", repoId, localPath);
227:             return FileUtil.del(repoDir);
228: 
229:         } finally {
230:             lock.unlock();
231:         }
232:     }
233: 
234:     @Override
235:     public void fetchBranches(Long repoId, String authUrl, List<String> branchNames) {
236:         if (branchNames == null || branchNames.isEmpty()) {
237:             return;
238:         }
239:         ReentrantLock lock = getRepoLock(repoId);
240:         lock.lock();
241:         try {
242:             String localPath = getLocalRepositoryPath(repoId);
243:             File repoDir = new File(localPath);
244:             if (!repoDir.exists() || !new File(repoDir, ".git").exists()) {
245:                 throw Errors.INTERNAL_ERROR.toException("Local repository does not exist: " + localPath);
246:             }
247:             CredentialsProvider credentials = parseCredentials(authUrl);
248:             List<RefSpec> refSpecs = new ArrayList<>();
249:             for (String branch : branchNames) {
250:                 refSpecs.add(new RefSpec("refs/heads/" + branch + ":refs/heads/" + branch));
251:             }
252:             try (Git git = Git.open(repoDir)) {
253:                 FetchCommand fetch = git.fetch()
254:                         .setRemote("origin")
255:                         .setRefSpecs(refSpecs)
256:                         .setTimeout(gitProperties.getTimeoutSeconds());
257:                 if (credentials != null) {
258:                     fetch.setCredentialsProvider(credentials);
259:                 }
260:                 fetch.call();
261:                 log.info("Fetch branches completed: repoId={}, branches={}", repoId, branchNames.size());
262:             }
263:         } catch (IOException | GitAPIException e) {
264:             log.error("Failed to fetch branches: repoId={}, error={}", repoId, e.getMessage(), e);
265:             throw Errors.INTERNAL_ERROR.toException("Failed to fetch branches: " + e.getMessage());
266:         } finally {
267:             lock.unlock();
268:         }
269:     }
270: 
271:     @Override
272:     public List<String> listRemoteBranches(String authUrl) {
273:         try {
274:             CredentialsProvider credentials = parseCredentials(authUrl);
275:             Collection<Ref> refs = Git.lsRemoteRepository()
276:                     .setRemote(authUrl)
277:                     .setCredentialsProvider(credentials)
278:                     .setHeads(true)
279:                     .setTags(false)
280:                     .setTimeout(gitProperties.getTimeoutSeconds())
281:                     .call();
282: 
283:             return refs.stream()
284:                     .map(ref -> Repository.shortenRefName(ref.getName()))
285:                     .sorted()
286:                     .collect(Collectors.toList());
287:         } catch (GitAPIException e) {
288:             log.error("Failed to get remote branch list: error={}", e.getMessage(), e);
289:             throw Errors.INTERNAL_ERROR.toException("Failed to get remote branch list: " + e.getMessage());
290:         }
291:     }
292: 
293:     /// Get repository operation lock
294:     private ReentrantLock getRepoLock(Long repoId) {
295:         return repoLocks.computeIfAbsent(repoId, k -> new ReentrantLock());
296:     }
297: 
298:     /// Parse authentication information
299:     private CredentialsProvider parseCredentials(String authUrl) {
300:         // Parse token from URL
301:         // Format: https://oauth2:token@host/path or https://token@host/path
302:         try {
303:             java.net.URL url = new java.net.URL(authUrl);
304:             String userInfo = url.getUserInfo();
305:             if (userInfo != null && !userInfo.isEmpty()) {
306:                 String[] parts = userInfo.split(":");
307:                 if (parts.length >= 2) {
308:                     // oauth2:token format
309:                     return new UsernamePasswordCredentialsProvider(parts[0], parts[1]);
310:                 } else {
311:                     // Token-only format
312:                     return new UsernamePasswordCredentialsProvider(userInfo, "");
313:                 }
314:             }
315:         } catch (java.net.MalformedURLException e) {
316:             log.warn("Failed to parse URL: {}", authUrl);
317:         }
318:         return null;
319:     }
320: 
321:     /// Get latest commit
322:     private RevCommit getLatestCommit(Git git) throws IOException {
323:         try {
324:             Iterable<RevCommit> commits = git.log().setMaxCount(1).call();
325:             for (RevCommit commit : commits) {
326:                 return commit;
327:             }
328:         } catch (GitAPIException e) {
329:             log.warn("Failed to get latest commit: {}", e.getMessage());
330:         }
331:         return null;
332:     }
333: }

parts.text:
Yes — self-hosted GitLab is supported.

- The app treats GitLab by parsing API base from the repo `webUrl`, so `https://gitlab.your-org.local/group/repo` works (not limited to `gitlab.com`) (`server/src/main/java/com/mzfuture/entire/gitsync/adapter/GitlabAdapter.java:62`).
- Sync/clone logic is generic and uses that URL + token, so the rest of features (checkpoints, conversations, charts) work the same once sync succeeds (`server/src/main/java/com/mzfuture/entire/gitsync/service/impl/GitSyncServiceImpl.java:96`).

For self-signed certs: not out-of-the-box unless the cert is trusted by JVM.

- There is currently no “skip SSL verify” toggle in code for token validation or JGit operations.
- Token validation uses Java `HttpClient` and will fail on untrusted certs (`server/src/main/java/com/mzfuture/entire/gitsync/adapter/GitlabAdapter.java:65`).
- Clone/pull/fetch uses JGit, which also relies on JVM trust settings (`server/src/main/java/com/mzfuture/entire/gitsync/service/impl/GitOperationServiceImpl.java:73`).

What to do in your org setup:
1. Import your GitLab CA/self-signed cert into the JVM truststore used by `entire-dashboard` (host/container), then restart.
2. Or use an internal CA cert already trusted by that JVM.
3. (Not recommended) plain HTTP GitLab in isolated network.

If you want, I can give you exact Docker Compose + `keytool` commands for your current deployment.

118:         // Incremental walk by branch: each branch uses its own lastProcessedCommitSha as stopAtSha
119:         List<CheckpointGitReader.CommitInfo> allCommits = new ArrayList<>();
120:         for (String branchName : contentBranches) {
121:             String stopAtSha = fullScan ? null : getLastProcessedCommitSha(repoId, branchName);
122:             log.debug("Checkpoint sync: processing branch {}, repoId={}", branchName, repoId);
123:             List<CheckpointGitReader.CommitInfo> commits = gitReader.walkCommitsFromBranch(repoId, branchName, stopAtSha);
124:             allCommits.addAll(commits);
125:             log.debug("Checkpoint sync: branch {} done, commits={}, repoId={}", branchName, commits.size(), repoId);
126:         }
127:         Collections.reverse(allCommits);
128: 
129:         for (CheckpointGitReader.CommitInfo commit : allCommits) {
130:             String checkpointId = parseTrailer(commit.fullMessage());
131:             if (checkpointId == null) {
132:                 continue;
133:             }
134:             String path = checkpointId.substring(0, 2) + "/" + checkpointId.substring(2) + "/metadata.json";
135:             Optional<String> metaOpt = gitReader.getFileContent(repoId, metadataRevision, path);
136:             if (metaOpt.isEmpty()) {
137:                 log.debug("Checkpoint metadata not found: repoId={}, commit={}, path={}", repoId, commit.commitSha(), path);
138:                 continue;
139:             }
140:             String metaJson = metaOpt.get();
141:             CheckpointParseResult parsed = metadataParser.parseCheckpointMetadata(metaJson);
142:             if (parsed == null || parsed.getCheckpointId() == null) {
143:                 continue;
144:             }
145:             String lastSessionPath = metadataParser.getLastSessionMetadataPath(metaJson);
146:             if (StrUtil.isNotBlank(lastSessionPath)) {
147:                 Optional<String> sessionMeta = gitReader.getFileContent(repoId, metadataRevision, lastSessionPath);
148:                 parsed.setAgent(sessionMeta.map(metadataParser::parseSessionAgent).orElse(null));
149:             }
150:             CheckpointGitReader.LineStats lineStats = gitReader.getCommitLineStats(repoId, commit.commitSha()).orElse(null);
151:             Checkpoint savedCheckpoint = upsertCheckpoint(repoId, commit.commitSha(), commit.fullMessage(), commit.authorName(), commit.commitTime(), parsed, lineStats);
152:             upsertSessionsForCheckpoint(repoId, metadataRevision, metaJson, savedCheckpoint.getId());
153:         }
154: 
155:         // Consistency: update state by branch only after all success (within transaction, any step failure rolls back entirely)
156:         long now = System.currentTimeMillis();
157:         for (String branchName : contentBranches) {
158:             String headSha = gitReader.resolveBranchCommitSha(repoId, branchName).orElse(null);
159:             if (headSha != null) {
160:                 updateSyncStateSuccess(repoId, branchName, headSha, now);
161:             }
162:         }
163:         // Update repository table last successful sync time
164:         repo.setLastSuccessfulSyncAt(now);
165:         repoRepository.save(repo);
166:         log.info("Checkpoint sync done: repoId={}, contentBranches={}, processed={}", repoId, contentBranches.size(), allCommits.size());
167:     }
168: 
169:     @Override
170:     public void syncAllRepos(boolean fullScan) {
171:         List<Repo> repos = repoRepository.findAll();
172:         for (Repo repo : repos) {
173:             try {
174:                 syncRepo(repo.getId(), fullScan);
175:             } catch (Exception e) {
176:                 log.error("Checkpoint sync failed for repoId={}: {}", repo.getId(), e.getMessage(), e);
177:             }
178:         }
179:     }
180: 
181:     private boolean remoteHasBranch(Long repoId, String branch) {
182:         GitBranchesDTO dto = gitSyncService.getBranches(repoId);
183:         return dto.getBranches() != null && dto.getBranches().contains(branch);
184:     }
185: 
186:     private String parseTrailer(String fullMessage) {
187:         if (fullMessage == null) {
188:             return null;
189:         }
190:         Matcher m = TRAILER_PATTERN.matcher(fullMessage);
191:         String last = null;
192:         while (m.find()) {
193:             last = m.group(1);
194:         }
195:         return last;
196:     }
197: 
198:     private String getLastProcessedCommitSha(Long repoId, String branch) {
199:         return syncStateRepository.findByRepoIdAndBranch(repoId, branch)
200:                 .map(CheckpointSyncState::getLastProcessedCommitSha)
201:                 .orElse(null);
202:     }
203: 
204:     @Transactional
205:     protected Checkpoint upsertCheckpoint(Long repoId, String commitSha, String commitMessage, String commitAuthorName, long commitTime, CheckpointParseResult parsed, CheckpointGitReader.LineStats lineStats) {
206:         Checkpoint entity = checkpointRepository.findByRepoIdAndCheckpointId(repoId, parsed.getCheckpointId())
207:                 .orElse(new Checkpoint());
208:         Repo repo = repoRepository.findById(repoId).orElse(null);
209:         entity.setCheckpointId(parsed.getCheckpointId());
210:         entity.setRepoId(repoId);
211:         entity.setRepoName(repo != null ? repo.getName() : null);
212:         entity.setBranch(parsed.getBranch());
213:         entity.setCommitSha(commitSha);
214:         entity.setCommitMessage(commitMessage);
215:         entity.setCommitAuthorName(commitAuthorName);
216:         entity.setCommitTime(commitTime);
217:         entity.setCheckpointsCount(parsed.getCheckpointsCount());
218:         entity.setFilesTouched(parsed.getFilesTouched());
219:         entity.setAdditions(lineStats != null ? lineStats.additions() : null);
220:         entity.setDeletions(lineStats != null ? lineStats.deletions() : null);
221:         entity.setTokenUsage(parsed.getTokenUsage());
222:         entity.setAgent(parsed.getAgent());
223:         return checkpointRepository.save(entity);
224:     }
225: 
226:     /// Upsert sessions for a checkpoint from metadata branch. Sessions array only (tasks/ excluded).
227:     private void upsertSessionsForCheckpoint(Long repoId, String metadataRevision, String checkpointMetaJson, Long checkpointId) {
228:         List<String> metadataPaths = metadataParser.getSessionMetadataPaths(checkpointMetaJson);
229:         for (int i = 0; i < metadataPaths.size(); i++) {
230:             String metaPath = metadataPaths.get(i);
231:             Optional<String> metaOpt = gitReader.getFileContent(repoId, metadataRevision, metaPath);
232:             if (metaOpt.isEmpty()) {
233:                 log.debug("Session metadata not found: repoId={}, path={}", repoId, metaPath);
234:                 continue;
235:             }
236:             SessionParseResult parsed = sessionMetadataParser.parse(metaOpt.get());
237:             if (parsed == null) {
238:                 continue;
239:             }
240:             parsed.setSessionIndex(i);
241:             String promptPath = metadataParser.getSessionPromptPath(checkpointMetaJson, i);
242:             String promptPreview = null;
243:             if (StrUtil.isNotBlank(promptPath)) {
244:                 Optional<String> promptOpt = gitReader.getFileContent(repoId, metadataRevision, promptPath);
245:                 promptPreview = sessionMetadataParser.getFirstLineOfPrompt(promptOpt.orElse(null));
246:             }
247:             parsed.setPromptPreview(promptPreview);
248: 
249:             Session session = sessionRepository.findByCheckpointIdAndSessionIndex(checkpointId, i).orElse(new Session());
250:             session.setCheckpointId(checkpointId);
251:             session.setSessionIndex(i);
252:             copyParseResultToSession(parsed, session);
253:             sessionRepository.save(session);
254:         }
255:     }
256: 
257:     private void copyParseResultToSession(SessionParseResult from, Session to) {
258:         to.setSessionId(from.getSessionId());
259:         to.setStrategy(from.getStrategy());
260:         to.setSessionCreatedAt(from.getSessionCreatedAt());
261:         to.setBranch(from.getBranch());
262:         to.setCheckpointsCount(from.getCheckpointsCount());
263:         to.setFilesTouchedCount(from.getFilesTouchedCount());
264:         to.setFilesTouchedJson(from.getFilesTouchedJson());
265:         to.setAgent(from.getAgent());
266:         to.setInputTokens(from.getInputTokens());
267:         to.setOutputTokens(from.getOutputTokens());
268:         to.setApiCallCount(from.getApiCallCount());
269:         to.setAgentLines(from.getAgentLines());
270:         to.setHumanAdded(from.getHumanAdded());
271:         to.setHumanModified(from.getHumanModified());
272:         to.setHumanRemoved(from.getHumanRemoved());
273:         to.setTotalCommitted(from.getTotalCommitted());
274:         to.setAgentPercentage(from.getAgentPercentage());
275:         to.setPromptPreview(from.getPromptPreview());
276:     }
277: 
278:     private void updateSyncStateSuccess(Long repoId, String branch, String lastProcessedCommitSha, long now) {
279:         CheckpointSyncState state = syncStateRepository.findByRepoIdAndBranch(repoId, branch).orElseGet(() -> {
280:             CheckpointSyncState s = new CheckpointSyncState();
281:             s.setRepoId(repoId);
282:             s.setBranch(branch);
283:             s.setCreatedAt(now);
284:             return s;
285:         });
286:         state.setLastProcessedCommitSha(lastProcessedCommitSha);
287:         state.setLastSyncAt(now);
288:         state.setLastError(null);
289:         state.setUpdatedAt(now);
290:         syncStateRepository.save(state);
291:     }
292: }

parts.text:
Implemented — you can now add a local filesystem Git repo from a separate tab in the same Create Repository slideover, and sync uses the same pipeline.

- Added a **Local Git** tab in `Create Repository`, with local-path input and remote/local-specific validation/fields in `web/app/features/repository/components/RepoCreateSlideover.vue`.
- Kept edit flow compatible with local repos (local path label, hides token for LOCAL) in `web/app/features/repository/components/RepoEditSlideover.vue`.
- Updated repo list rendering so local paths are shown as plain text (not clickable URL anchors) in `web/app/features/repository/components/RepoList.vue`.
- Added `LOCAL` platform support end-to-end: enum + sync adapter + tokenless handling in backend (`server/src/main/java/com/mzfuture/entire/gitrepo/enums/RepositoryPlatform.java`, `server/src/main/java/com/mzfuture/entire/gitsync/adapter/LocalAdapter.java`, `server/src/main/java/com/mzfuture/entire/gitsync/service/impl/GitSyncServiceImpl.java`).
- Relaxed URL-only DTO validation and added platform-aware backend validation (HTTP(S) URL for remote, absolute existing git path for LOCAL) in `server/src/main/java/com/mzfuture/entire/gitrepo/service/impl/RepoServiceImpl.java`, `server/src/main/java/com/mzfuture/entire/gitrepo/dto/request/RepoCreateParams.java`, and `server/src/main/java/com/mzfuture/entire/gitrepo/dto/request/RepoUpdateParams.java`.
- Added duplicate URL/path check on update in `server/src/main/java/com/mzfuture/entire/gitrepo/repository/RepoRepository.java`.

Validation I ran:
- `pnpm build` in `web/` passed.
- Backend compile could not be run in this environment because Java runtime is missing.

If you want, next I can add an explicit “Path not found / not a git repo” inline helper in the Create tab before submit (currently backend gives the definitive error).

#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

SERVER_PORT=8080
WEB_PORT=3001

stop_by_port() {
  local port="$1"
  local name="$2"
  local pids

  pids="$(lsof -tiTCP:"$port" -sTCP:LISTEN || true)"
  if [ -n "$pids" ]; then
    printf 'Stopping %s on port %s (PID: %s)\n' "$name" "$port" "$pids"
    kill $pids
  else
    printf '%s not running on port %s.\n' "$name" "$port"
  fi
}
